title: poa nemoralis subsp. glauca
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. glauca, also known as the wood meadow-grass or pale meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the wood meadow-grass plant (Poa nemoralis) and a type of grass native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis subsp. glauca include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
